subject: Resolve crash around picking?
body:
If picking is enabled, Warp reliably crashes while showing the "loading 
boxnet model"

It does not write any crash logs.


I've tried with Warp 1.0.7W (and 1.0.6),
on two (nearly identical) machines, windows 7,
initially with a GTX 960 (2GB), just updated drivers because that was my 
first suspect. and currently with a 11GB GTX 1080Ti because I figured it 
might be amount of graphics memory but the crash is the same.


I'm trying to eliminate graphics memory as a possibility, so I have 
quesitons:
- how do I tell decisively? :)
- is the 8GB graphics memory mentioned on the site a 
you-want-this-for-speed thing, or a hard non-crashy requirement?
- does that requirement relate to just motion correction, or also the 
tensorflow stuff?
- does it relate to micrograph size or not (I assume it does for 
motioncorr, but does it also for the picking case)? 


Window's own crash details:

Problem signature:
  Problem Event Name: APPCRASH
  Application Name: Warp.exe
  Application Version: 1.0.8.0
  Application Timestamp: 5d9cbc15
  Fault Module Name: ucrtbase.DLL
  Fault Module Version: 10.0.10586.1171
  Fault Module Timestamp: 59ae5010
  Exception Code: 40000015
  Exception Offset: 00000000000699df
  OS Version: 6.1.7601.2.1.0.256.48
  Locale ID: 1033
  Additional Information 1: d5de
  Additional Information 2: d5de7ea96b146c2e34116ba80f3c3122
  Additional Information 3: 0aa6
  Additional Information 4: 0aa6c98ba1856a42c735eba87c82aaa9


I've tried installing VS to see if I could get a debugger working but when 
attached, it seems to ask for debug stuff I don't have.



I don't really know what I've eliminated or nto.direction to look in.

Anyone have similar experience?
